prompt: The service's startup capability line is intended to give operators one stable account of what was compiled in, what became usable at runtime, and why anything was disabled. It currently rediscovers accelerator state while formatting the line, so startup probes a device twice and the log includes discovery diagnostics that can contain process-specific pointers and host-specific paths. Repair the reporting boundary so it summarizes the results already produced by the existing startup sequence. Preserve the accelerator-before-telemetry initialization order and emit the summary only after those decisions are complete. Use stable capability names, runtime states, and disabled-reason tokens; never include device addresses, device paths, or telemetry destinations. Distinguish not-compiled, configuration-disabled, probe failures, and initialization failures. Do not change the public component interfaces or the protected tests. Commands must remain offline and deterministic. Verification is `bash tests/run_tests.sh` from the repository root.

full_distill.primary_skill: separate startup observation from discovery by carrying stable capability outcomes through the existing C++ initialization sequence
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected startup test observes a second accelerator probe and a capability line containing a raw device address and diagnostic path
full_distill.success_contract: one stable summary line reports compile-time and runtime state plus canonical disabled reasons, each device is probed once, and accelerator initialization still precedes telemetry initialization and reporting
adversarial_factors: the probe records every call, so re-querying it only for logging is observable even when it returns the same device
successful discovery provides both a real object address and a host-shaped diagnostic path, neither of which may leak into the summary
separate scenarios pin permission, absence, configuration, initialization-failure, and not-compiled reason tokens
an exact event trace rejects fixes that move reporting earlier or reorder accelerator and telemetry initialization
